response.justification: Bogdan brings genuine geospatial domain depth (founder of the GISCollective GIS platform, Green Map work) and real low-latency distributed-systems experience handling millions of requests per second, but that systems work was in DLang over a decade ago, and his hands-on C++ dates to 2007-2008 with no Rust to compensate. His recent years are TypeScript/Node full-stack product work, which leaves the role's core requirement — modern C++ on a production routing data pipeline — unevidenced, and he has no navigation guidance or routing-tile experience specifically.
strengths: Deep geospatial domain experience: founded and still maintains an open-source GIS/geo-data platform used by Green Map
Proven low-latency distributed-systems work (async I/O, fibers, caching) on infrastructure handling millions of HTTP requests per second
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, Linux, Docker, and a track record of adapting across stacks and languages
concerns: No modern C++ or Rust experience; last commercial C++ work was 2007-2008
No routing, navigation guidance, or map-tile pipeline experience despite adjacent GIS background
Recent decade is predominantly web/full-stack TypeScript product work, distant from systems-level pipeline engineering
